review_text: Camped at this lovely spot for over 29 years and its always been our favourite, although alot more busy now that the secret is out. However, i must say I was a little disappointed last vist. Now i am not one to complain and i leave my fair share of positve reviews where credit is due but this was so disappointing.The amenities building (which has been that same for the last 20yrs) was in a bad state and not enough amenitiesfor the amount of people using them. Normally they are very clean in good shape. This time it was disgusting. The septic must have been over full and smelled terrible. The concrete was all warped and water was pooling and not draining away whichever soon turned into a pool of mud. I thought they might have build a new block by now or at least have some portable toilets down lower level (like they used to have). The check in building, which is a lovely old shack with such character is all rotten and slowly falling apart and looks terrible. Seems like all the improvements have gone to the new cabins and privately hired venue areas. Hopefully management reads this review and does something about it. It's such a beautiful spot.
Great place to just sit back and 😌 Relax

Awesome spot on the Murray River. Camp site are first in best dressed, we snagged a spot next to the river overlooking the river and the hills nearby. Highly recommend if you are fully self sufficient. Great off grid spot

We had a fantastic stay at Kenilworth Homestead over the Easter long weekend. It’s such a great spot for families—there are heaps of activities included in the camp fees which kept everyone entertained, especially the kids. From the kids disco, flying fox, pony rides and petting zoo to open space to explore, it really makes for an easy and enjoyable family getaway. These activities were all included in our camp fees. There were also lots of food vans for families to buy great food.

One of the highlights was definitely the river—perfect for swimming and relaxing during the day. It’s a beautiful setting and adds so much to the overall experience.

The only downside we found was around the quiet hours at night. Unfortunately, one family had loud music and drunken noise going until around 1am each night, which made it hard to fully relax after a big day. It might be worth management reviewing and enforcing quiet hours a bit more strictly to keep the experience enjoyable for all guests.

Overall though, a great place for a family camping trip—we’d definitely come back!
Always nice here....
This was our 5th visit and enjoyed the serenity again. The advantage of visiting on a work week is less people. The property is maintained wonderfully and our dogs loved having a good swim..
not recommended if you require clean amenities and a camp kitchen. I
not a good hideout for ww3, great camping tho
Love camping here so peaceful.
Didn’t stay but the receptionist was very helpful and I will be staying here in the future because they made that simple effort to be nice and helpful. Oh and the views are spectacular.
Fantastic camping area.
Went for a few nights , couldn't recommend this enough

So much to see!! Always love coming to Kenilworth. Usually, we book Kenilworth Camping. However, that was booked out for the King's Birthday long weekend.

I was amazing that this place wasn't booked out yet. Managed to get in for the weekend and I am truly happy with the way this venue is sit up.

Amazing river swimming, amenities, BMX track if you want to bring your bike and amazing host.

Thanks Kenilworth Homestead.
It's OK but not enough amenities very small
Good clean camping, lots of sites to choose from.

Nice campsite
Just a little too open for my liking
Toilets are a decent trek from where we camped at least a 20-minute walk
Beautiful surroundings Mary River running through it.
Would definitely recommend and come back
Great spot-  as long as it isn't Easter long weekend! Have gone every year for years, great water holes and not bad kayaking!

Nice peaceful spot. Amenities were clean and tidy.
Had a good bike track for the kids, which kept them occupied for hours.
Nice place.
Great spot, facilities as expected.

Nice and quiet, close to town, well maintained and friendly staff.
had a nice relaxing time and will be going back.

Fantastic place to stay for a few nights of peace and aahhhh the serenity, showers could do with a bit of looking at as water just dribbles from them when more than one is turned on but overall well worth a visit peoples ...

I honestly can’t believe somewhere like this is still allowed to operate. Camp Kenilworth Homestead was an absolute disaster from the moment we arrived. The grounds looked completely neglected, with overgrown grass, random rubbish just scattered around, and broken fences that looked like they hadn’t been fixed in years.

The cabins and camp facilities were basic at best and dirty at worst. The toilets and showers were grim—hair clogged drains, cracked tiles, and no proper cleaning at all. The sinks barely had running water, and the hot‑water system seemed to be broken half the time. It felt more like a construction site than a campsite.

The staff were rude and uninterested. When you asked for help, they either ignored you or gave short, dismissive answers. There was no sense of safety briefing, no clear information where anything was, and zero supervision for younger kids. It felt chaotic and disorganised.

The food was bland, overpriced, and clearly the cheapest option they could get away with. Portions were small, and there was no catering for allergies or different dietary needs. The activities were poorly run or just cancelled without warning, and the supposed ‘campfire nights’ were basically everyone sitting in the cold while no one in charge actually did anything.

For the price people paid, this was completely unacceptable. I expected it to be basic, but I didn’t expect it to be unsafe, unclean, and badly managed. I would strongly advise anyone to stay far away from this place and choose a proper, well‑run camp.
Place has turned to 💩 since new owners/caretakers have taken over.
Awesome campground.
Our visit was in April, we woke to the whole campsite covered in mist what a beautiful way to start the day

Awsome over Easter long weekend lots of extra toilets and they cleaned and restocked twice a day excellent service by the owners heaps to do for the kids the Marry River was beautiful as always to swim in

Stunning campsite, well maintained grass on a beautiful river. My kids loved SUP on the water. Third time we have stayed and won’t be back until amenities are significantly upgraded- seriously too few showers for numbers of campers and filthy all weekend. Also so noisy till 11pm so our young children struggled to sleep.
Amazing stay.

Nice location and friendly staff, but definitely needs more toilets, especially during the busier periods. Toilets were disgusting over the long weekend and there were not enough to accommodate everyone. Amenities block also needs a revamp.
Amenities dont get cleaned and need a major refurb. Disgusting.

The Amenity block is badly lacking, HOWEVER - the access to the river is superb to cool off - the dogs had a fabulous time but it was surpassed by the helpfulness and friendliness of the staff. The little guy in the 4 wheel drive buggy is amazing. He outshines any negatives with his sense of humour and can do attitude.  A real asset to the business
just stayed here for the first time, had a awesome 3 days with kids, toilets and facilities were clean, and the buggy man was a really nice friendly guy will definitely be back
Beautiful campsite and landscape! Everything was very well maintained, clean bathrooms, and hot showers. I would definitely return !
Has a great little day trip to the homestead. Park was well maintained and tidy. Would definitely camp here in the future.

As I was on a school camp, we experienced one of the worst camps I have ever been to. The staff there were nice, especially Zoe, the new one. However, on the hike, we had the most horrifying instructors on this planet, Tara. She tried to burn me with a fire pan thing, made me and my friends do the work, all of it including everything, and she was constantly rude as she complained that we were being brats, although we were in the most pain you could ever experienced, from the 15km hike she forced us to complete without breaks, uphill all the way. Everyone else went the short way. Like are you kidding me???????????????
Ur epic Zoe! This is Trisha's friend, my opinion on this camp was kind of mutual. The camp instructor I had was horrible he didnt help us at all just sat there and told us what to do in a really bad explanation. This is the reason dinner took 3 WHOLE HOURS to cook. We had to figure everything out ourselves. Other than that my experience was really good. Be aware of camp food, prepare yourself.
Dih🥀💔

Beautiful camp ground, very friendly and Daen on the buggy is an absolute legend bringing firewood to us, and having a chat.

It's that type of service that makes all the difference.

We will be back!!!

Anita & Andrew

Beautiful camp ground, very friendly and quiet. ||Its a relaxing place to stay and Daen on the buggy is an absolute legend bringing firewood to us, and having a chat.||It's that type of service that makes all the difference.||We will be back!!!||Anita & Andrew
The man at the booking was so rude wouldn't stay for free went down the road much better

Loved the experience it was quiet and peaceful had no issues with campers or noise such a beautiful place to escape the rat race|The town was a very short drive from the camp grounds |Loved the scenery around the area and the little bakery |People were very friendly
ernest
Great camping area
Love this place every time we go.

Kenilworth Homestead - 4 Stars

We stayed at Kenilworth Homestead over the Easter weekend, which was incredibly busy with a big crowd. Overall, it was a beautiful experience, but there were a couple of things that could be improved.

The biggest downside was the shower situation. There were only 3 showers for the ladies and 3 for the men to cater for what felt like around 1000 people. This led to long wait times, and unfortunately, the showers were very unclean. Only 2 of the female showers were working properly, which made it frustrating. Definitely something that could use more attention during peak times.

That said, we had no issues with overcrowding or space — we camped down the back along the Mary River and found there was plenty of room. We did arrive 7am to snag a good spot. It seemed really well spread out. More porta loos were brought in and were easily accessible across the campground, and they were kept clean with toilet paper stocked all weekend, which was great to see.

The Homestead organised a lot of activities and food trucks, which were a big hit with everyone. The beach area along the Mary River was absolutely stunning — I’ve never seen such clear water in a river before. It was perfect for a hot weekend. Our family brought blowup boats and sailed down the river, and we highly recommend bringing something if you want to do the same.

Ice was always available at reception (which was a lifesaver!) and the town is only a short drive away if you need anything extra.

Constructive feedback: It would be amazing to see a few more taps closer to the campsites, and definitely some extra showers brought in during busier times.

Overall, Kenilworth Homestead is a beautiful spot, and we had a great weekend. We would definitely stay again.

My wife has cancer and she wanted to getaway for a couple of days so we spent 2 nights in the spa room.
The tv had no reception and we couldn't log into the WiFi. I did ask for the password they said they didn't have it. On Google maps it says free parking and WiFi
The sky light in the room let's too Much light in when the sun rises. There is a swimming pool but you can't use it. We would not recommend staying here not worth the $230 price per night . And there was no dishwashing liquid to clean the dishes .
Always a favourite place to stay. We been going there for over 20 years.

Great place for family camping trip we go every year our sons and grandkids camp we go in the spa rooms they are lovely it's beautiful sitting or swimming in the Mary River very clean great family fun would highly recommend it 😀

The amenities were clean and the camp ground was so picturesque. We stayed Friday and checked out Sunday It rained so much over the weekend but it did not spoil the stay.||We will definitely be staying again !  I had read some reviews prior to staying and had some concerns. I contacted the homestead they were very quick at replying and put my mind at rest.  Even if you see the bad review please still give the place a go !
Very lush.  couldn't ask for much more. If you chasing a quick getaway. Heaps todo places to swim an fish.

Great place to take kids swim in the creek or take a canoe some pony's,donkeys and a alpaca for the kids to pat. Open fires permitted some powered sites limited but ample sites for campers. Lots of grass areas for kids to ride bikes etc also a bmx track.

We enjoyed staying at this camping with a group of families. It feels very safe for kids and they have horses and donkies for kids to feed. The place has also a creek where guest can swim. We would go back.

We have been coming to the homestead for years camping . It has always been a great family spot bringing our girls here which they love . It is one of our favourite spots at Kenilworth to camp . Staff are always lovely happy to help with any questions , when it’s a busy time they always bring in extra toilets which are kept clean and empty. The guy on the buggy is always patrolling and making sure there is no issues at all . Yes the shower block that are here need to have work done to them and another built would be great but they are always clean and if there are any issue we have let staff know and it has always been fixed .

We love how there is enough space at the river for everyone and also with Easter they put on activities for the children and also big kids aka adults .

We will continue coming here for years to come .

Beautiful location beside the Mary river. We came on the Kings birthday public holiday along with a 1,000 odd others and it was fantastic. Toilets were cleaned and re stocked daily, the behaviour of all visitors was great with staff driving around checking on things. Great family getaway and beautiful campgrounds. We didn’t use the amenities so I can’t comment on that. Great it you have your shower, or just swim in the river. We will be back.
We went camping, it was fantastic.  There are showers and toilets.

Stayed 5 nights camping, campground is beautiful and so is the location. Big let down is the amenities doesn’t get cleaned regularly, rundown, way to small and lack of space for dressing little kids or putting stuff to keep dry. Unfortunately would not return unless they make much needed upgrades to amenities. Also the shop/office is pretty ordinary seemed to be closed more then opened had barely anything.

Easy drive North of Brisbane. Arrive after lunch on a Friday with good options to set up camp. Chose the river and it didn't disappoint, the campground manager person made frequent rounds and was always done for a chat. Campers next to us played exceedingly loud music till 11pm which impacted the entire campground. (Seems to be the norm these days, can't camp without making it sound like a club). Did ask them to turn it off at no noise hour, but was ignored. Portaloos ran out of toilet paper late Saturday, and ran out of water on Sunday morning. Will go back but be more selective about where we setup up. The campground manager asked us how our neighbours were in the morning during his rounds, we said loud and late, and he had a chat with them, but did little I imagine - it was around 7:30am and they were playing music already. Muppets.

A lovely camping site to take your dogs and also swim. The depth of the water was just perfect. Hope it remains the same in the summer too.
The only issue is that there is no designated area for campers and it's difficult to find privacy and also fire pits
Best experience ever 🗣🗣🗣

I have never been more disappointed with service or lack of service that this place has provided - and I have been camping in loads of different places all around Australia for 50 years.

We were wanting a camping spot for my daughter's 18th for a bunch of young people. We decided on Kenilworth Homestead because we've stayed there before, it's close, pretty and you can have a fire. Four of us booked and paid in full via their website for 4-7/7. We decided to drive out there to have a look where we should camp and were horrified to find a sign on the gate saying they were closed until 16 August. We had been trying to call with no answer for days prior to the drive - clearly because they were closed.

We wanted to cancel and get a refund because they weren't going to be open, so our booking wasn't going to be honored. They have been absolutely appalling and won't give us a refund. They will only do a camp credit which is not acceptable for us.

We don't want to stay somewhere that unfairly advertised they were open, took our money under false pretenses and now won't provide a refund. They refunded my daughter and her boyfriend but won't refund me! What the hell??? They even tried to tell me it wasn't their fault that the wrong sign had been out on the gate!!! Sure as hell wasn't mine!

Add to that the terrible reviews that they are getting for their facilities, we are glad we decided not to go. They have had a week to refund me and still nothing. Small Claims tribunal and ACCC will be contacted tomorrow. Will never EVER recommend anyone to stay there again.

It's not even about the refund anymore for me - it's about the terrible customer service. They advertised a service, we paid for it, found that they weren't going to deliver that service and they won't give me money back even though they've already provided a refund for one of our group!!!

Seriously terrible, dreadful service!!! :(🤬🤬🤬
Absolutely beautiful part of the country
This place was such a blast for the kids! Highly recommend. Great property and well maintained. Will be back.
Highly recommended. Well maintained, great facilities, great spot to camp.

We have been camping at the homestead for many years now. Can confidently say the last few times we have camped there the more we get disappointed with the camp ground. Nice maintained grass area but the bathrooms are often very dirty and not often cleaned at all. Toilet paper often all over floor, rubbish, dirty toilets, grubby showers, over flowing bins in the bathrooms and often no paper towel to dry your hands. Very unhygienic bathrooms that's for sure. Definitely not the first time we have seen the bathroom be so filthy. Just spent 4 days there and not once did we see the bathrooms be cleaned.. the rubbish that was all over the bathroom floor and day 1 was still there on day 4. Very limited toilets and showers for the amount of people who camp there. Also couldnt safely let young children just run and play without having to worry they would get hit by a car. Way to much driving through the camp ground by campers driving to and from the toilet blocks. Also lots of cars speeding through the camp ground.

Won't be back in a hurry.
Such a great place to camp! Beautiful location and scenery.

If you are a camping beginner, this is the place you must go. Shower, toilet and tap water are all included.

Night time is amazing

If I could give zero stars I would. The biggest issue we have was the facilities where were horrifically inadequate for the number of people there. If you wanted a shower, be prepared to go at 2pm or wait for a ridiculous amount of time for the 3 showers available, of which hot water was mostly non existent and which were regularly decorated with thick mud on the floor. Adding to this, we were supplied with portaloos scattered across the site of probably 300+ families - these were rarely cleaned, often without toilet paper and by Monday morning they had only been pumped out once so they were so full of waste that they couldn’t flush.

We came as a large group as recommended by a friend and while we could see how they loved it when they had been previously, we were left a bit disappointed with Easter weekend. There were a range of kids activities for all ages, food trucks etc which were great. However, we were packed in far too closely in what felt more like a festival campsite than any kind of relaxing camping. There really needs to be a cap on numbers or assigned spaces because the bigger issue was amenities. We had 2 x portaloos for approximately 100 people, and the closest shower block had 3 toilets and 3 showers (not sure about men’s bathroom) with another amenities block  much further up with an additional few. For context there must have been 1000+ people there this weekend and a 40 minute wait for muddy showers (with almost no pressure and consistently going cold), especially with small children is just not acceptable.

Had we not been packed in like this, we would have made Kenilworth Homestead our new regular destination however we will continue to return to our usual regular instead.

love this place, but really need to improve on the amenities especially for the busy periods. showers with no pressure or heat aren't that great considering the costs during peak times. but it's lovely and peaceful and relaxing
Kenilworth how could you go wrong.  Great place

Won’t be back again, Owner or manager is a seriously rude person. If your a person with the same car as a teenager who’s running a muck, your getting the blame and getting kicked out. Even tho we weren’t with these people. He went straight to another family and had a go at them aswell, when they’ve done nothing wrong.
Convenient location but could do with more shade. Nice little creek though

As a regular camper I would have to say this is one of the Worst camp facilities around, Overpriced for the facilities. Amenities are in a state of disrepair, locks broken on every door, poor water pressure, Dirty toilets and sinks, they over book the campgrounds hence you are camping on top of each other. Four powered sites however 10 vans parked there. !! Overpriced for the facilities, they seriously need to spend a dollar on this place. Kenilworth is a beautiful town however we cannot recommend staying here.
Have had multiple trips here. It a lovely family friendly place! Highly recommend!
Absolutely Beautiful place and great host and great location. Heaps of room and lots of trees and a creek to swim in. Dog friendly too
Great quiet place

Had a great time doing nothing but relaxing. Convenient and close to town, we could spread out on this large property without impacting other groups and have a great time. Plenty of places to swim in the river, bike track to keep kids amused and clean facilities. We like coming here.
Ron has the worst customer service skills I’ve ever experienced

We recently stayed at the homestead for the first time for four nights. On the first couple of nights over the weekend was extremely busy and some people where quite loud and not considerate of other campers. Believe one group came in after hours and left before hours and were so noisy leaving glass and cans around their spot disappointing for the people who come for a relaxing time and pay for their site and leave the place better than they arrive. Grounds are well maintained. We are self contained so no major issues for us but looking at the amenities for the quantity of people that use the park at any one time the amenities are minimal. Only three showers. No dump point for self contained and no water for self contained had to head into town. Free dump point at the show grounds which was handy. Dog friendly which was great. Everyone appears to clean up after their pets. Our dogs loved the place. Once the weekend campers left the last couple of days the place felt like you had the place almost to yourself. If you want space and serenity then come mid week for this place. Extremely busy for the weekend.
A lovely place to wind down and relax.
